commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether to restore the narrow redaction branch, split the broad second-commit surfaces, or explicitly authorize the release/dependency work; this is not a safe automatic repair while th...; Needs attention: The intended redaction change is security-positive, but the current PR also changes dependency graph and release automation surfaces unrelated to the stated fix.; Review finding:... (sha=eba68ed9d4837c261710169eb0e731bce0cc2c96)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds Fireworks `fw-`, `fw_`, and `fpk_` token redaction to the shared Node and browser Control UI redactors with tests, but the current head also includes broad unrelated release, dependency, workflow, mobile, plugin/channel, docs, and UI changes.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, but not from a live command in this read-only pass. Current main lacks Fireworks bare-token prefixes in both redaction corpora, and the linked issue gives synthetic calls against the affected Node and browser entry points.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide whether to restore the narrow redaction branch, split the broad second-commit surfaces, or explicitly authorize the release/dependency work; this is not a safe automatic repair while the PR is conflicting and maintainer-touched.

target.title: Redact bare Fireworks API keys
target.body:
﻿Closes #98225

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Fireworks users could have bare API key material shown unchanged in logs, tool output, or Control UI tool details when the key was not attached to a sensitive field name such as `FIREWORKS_API_KEY`.

## Why This Change Was Made

OpenClaw already redacts generic API-key assignments, auth headers, and many provider-specific raw token prefixes. Fireworks raw token prefixes were missing from the default Node redaction pattern set, the fast-path prefilter, and the browser-only Control UI display redactor.

This adds Fireworks `fw-...`, `fw_...`, and `fpk_...` token shapes with a non-identifier left boundary so short ordinary strings remain visible. It does not change Fireworks authentication or provider behavior.

## User Impact

Fireworks provider users get the same raw-token masking protection as other first-class provider users across logs and browser-rendered tool details. Short non-key strings such as `fw-tooshort`, `fw_tooshort`, and `fpk_tooshort` continue to render unchanged.

## Evidence

Runtime proof on PR commit `17c915e0b2dea8cbc6cd9006081b72e8fe953359`, using synthetic in-memory token strings only (`'fw-' + 'C'.repeat(40)`, `'fw_' + 'A'.repeat(40)`, and `'fpk_' + 'B'.repeat(40)`). No real credentials were used.

```text
Synthetic Fireworks redaction proof (no real secrets)
Node input generated token families: fw-, fw_, fpk_
Node output: provider error fw-CCC?CCCC fw_AAA?AAAA fpk_BB?BBBB
Node leaked raw token: false
Browser tool-detail output: openclaw onboard --provider fireworks fw-CCC...CCCC fw_AAA...AAAA fpk_BB...BBBB
Browser leaked raw token: false
```

The proof exercises the actual PR implementations of `redactSensitiveText(..., { mode: "tools" })` and the browser-only `redactToolDetail(...)` entrypoint.

Focused verification:

- `node scripts\run-vitest.mjs src\logging\redact.test.ts --testNamePattern="masks expanded vendor-prefix token corpus|does not redact ordinary identifiers containing short token-prefix substrings"`
  - `1 Test File passed`
  - `2 passed | 106 skipped`
- `node scripts\run-vitest.mjs ui\src\ui\browser-redact.test.ts --testNamePattern="redacts tool detail credential families without Node config imports"`
  - `1 Test File passed`
  - `1 passed | 1 skipped`
- `git diff --check`
- `$env:PYTHONUTF8='1'; python .agents\skills\autoreview\scripts\autoreview --mode local`
  - `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`
target.author: ooiuuii
